correct_answer: The Egyptians used hieroglyphics for written communication.
The development of writing systems led to the creation of various scripts and typefaces.
Johannes Gutenberg invented the printing press in the 15th century.
The printing press allowed for the mass production of books and printed materials.
The printing press made information more accessible to a wider audience.
Designers began to experiment with different fonts, sizes, and layouts for printed materials.
Claude Garamond and Giambattista Bodoni created iconic typefaces during the Renaissance period.
Typography evolved with an emphasis on aesthetics, readability, and functionality.
